immutable_record_notice: Once frozen, this study manifest is immutable. Changes require a new record version linked to the superseded raw-byte digest.

subject.name: SYNTHETIC-DRY-RUN kind-first and role-first taxonomy comparison
subject.definition: A constructed comparison subject containing two deliberately competing candidate organizations: Organization K assigns one series-stable primary functional kind, while Organization R assigns contextual, potentially many-valued functional roles.
subject.boundary: The subject is limited to deterministic serialization of constructed cases. It does not establish category truth, completeness, utility, biological validity, external validity, mechanism effectiveness, or adoption readiness.

competency_questions: Can the contract retain the different identity and cardinality commitments of Organization K and Organization R for every constructed boundary case?
Can the contract preserve explicit constraint failures and an unexecuted analysis without converting either into favorable evidence?
integrity_constraints: Each constructed case has one Organization K disposition and one Organization R disposition or an explicit NOT_DETERMINED value.
Organization K never records two primary kinds for one series without a split or declared primary kind.
Organization R never records a reproducible role without its declared context tuple.
A COMPLETED disposition is interpreted only as serialization execution and never as research evidence.
primary_method_profile.adjudication_method: Apply exact frozen strings and cardinality rules in deterministic case order; retain NOT_DETERMINED rather than infer missing content.
primary_method_profile.coverage_rule: Process every positive, negative, and boundary case for both candidate organizations and retain an entry for every case-organization pair.

research_question: Can the foundational-study contract serialize the distinct kind-first and role-first commitments across fixed positive, negative, and boundary constructions?

hypothesis.claim: The frozen fixture rules are sufficient to serialize an organization-specific disposition for every frozen constructed case.
hypothesis.competing_explanation: The organizations collapse under serialization because their different words do not produce different identity, cardinality, context, or classification commitments.
hypothesis.predicted_observation: Every case receives bounded dispositions and at least one boundary construction retains different Organization K and Organization R commitments.
hypothesis.falsifier: A case cannot be serialized without an undeclared inference, required coverage is absent, or every boundary case produces indistinguishable commitments.

analyses.question: Does the deterministic serialization retain a disposition for each case and distinct commitments where the constructed input declares them?
analyses.method: Traverse the frozen case order, apply each organization rule independently, and emit the exact identity, cardinality, context, and classification commitment strings.
analyses.decision_rule: Mark fixture serialization complete only if all eight cases have both organization entries; mark the fixture claim inconclusive if success depends on pre-authored expectations rather than independent observation.

analyses.question: Would independent adjudicators reproduce the constructed boundary dispositions under blinded application of the exact rules?
analyses.method: Obtain independent blinded classifications and preserve disagreements; the dry-run fixture intentionally provides no adjudicators and therefore must not execute this method.
analyses.decision_rule: Record NOT_RUN when no independent adjudicators or approved evaluation protocol are present; do not fabricate agreement values.

replication_criteria: A separate execution using the exact frozen raw bytes emits the same eight-case ledger in the same order.
The two organization rules are applied independently and missing context remains explicit.
The reproduction states that deterministic fixture completion is non-evidentiary.

rejection_criteria.condition: Any frozen case lacks either an Organization K or Organization R disposition.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Complete pairwise serialization is the exact bounded fixture claim.

rejection_criteria.condition: Any exact subject, profile, source, protocol, configuration, or manifest digest fails.
rejection_criteria.rationale: A mutated fixture cannot be interpreted as the frozen dry run.

rejection_criteria.condition: Processing requires inventing a missing context, label, or organization rule.
rejection_criteria.rationale: The fixture preserves unknowns rather than fabricating an evaluative result.

ethics_scope.rationale: Every case and output is artificial; no person, participant, user record, or human-derived dataset is used.

assets: Exact subject, method-profile, category-rule, case, protocol, output, and disposition bytes
threats: Over-interpretation of serialization completion as evidence
security_and_privacy_analysis.data_retention_and_deletion: Retain the full artificial bundle, including the NOT_RUN disposition. Regeneration replaces no frozen bytes and must create a new versioned family.
residual_risks: SHA-256 integrity does not prove authorship, semantic validity, independence, or scientific merit.
Readers may overlook the explicit non-evidentiary boundary.

limitations: The cases encode their expected organization commitments, so successful serialization is tautological contract coverage.
There is no natural, representative, independently authored, or statistically sampled case set.
The independent-adjudication analysis is intentionally NOT_RUN.
No scientific, novelty, adoption, validation, implementation, or mechanism maturity claim is supported.

mechanism_maturity_boundary.rationale: This is a mechanism-free synthetic taxonomy contract dry run.
